Title: MY Cards OCBC (Aggregator)
Content:
Last Update : Dec 30, 2025

MO PIC : Nora Atika

Xendit Stakeholders

Department

PIC

PIC Role

Partnership

Musa Sharuddin

Partnership PIC for Shopeepay

Product

Zian Wei Lee

Chief Product

Hazel Soon Yee Chiann

Online Card Payment

Partner PIC

Name

Email

Danial

AhmadDanialZaini@ocbc.com

Terence

TerenceLim@ocbc.com

Patrick

patrickleongkl@ocbc.com

Eligibility

Entity Type

Eligible

Local Domestic MY Entity Sdn Bhd / Bhd

✅

Local Domestic MY Entity Sole Prop

✅

Local Domestic MY Entity Association

✅

Local Domestic MY Entity LLP / Partnership

✅

Foreign Entity

❌

Local / Foreign Individual

❌


Merchant Requirements

Category

Requirements

Industry Restriction

Note: MO will coordinate with the Partnership department to negotiate with the partner for exceptions if sales are strongly pushing for a specific merchant.

(A) Restricted business

  • Internet Gambling (including betting, lottery, casino chips, wagers).

  • Prostitution and Pornography.

  • Real Estate Agency (excluding developers and property management/JMC).

  • Investment schemes and businesses offering counterfeit products.

  • Quasi-cash related: Money orders, wire transfers, cryptocurrency, foreign currency, and travelers cheques.

(B) High Risk (Not encouraged; high rejection likelihood)

  • Multi-level marketing (MLM)
    Time shares

  • Mail or Phone order (MOTO)-(Hospitality industry rated with 3 stars and above or deviation approval is needed with case to case basic)

  • Card not present transactions (excluding 3D and recurring).

  • Card not present merchant in the following merchant category codes(MCCs) are classified as High Band Risk (HBR)*:-

    • MCC 5962 - Direct Marketing—Travel-Related Arrangement Services

    • MCC 5966 - Direct Marketing—Outbound Telemarketing Merchants

    • MCC 5967 - Direct Marketing—Inbound Telemarketing Merchants

    • MCC 7273 - Dating Services

    • MCC 5122 - Drugs, Drug Proprietors, and Druggists Sundries

    • MCC 5912 - Drug Stores and Pharmacies

    • MCC 5993 - Cigar Stores and Stands

    • MCC 4816 - Computer Network/Information Services

(C) High Risk (Subject to approval)

  • Delay Delivery Service

  • Travel Agency

  • Fitness/Gym/Yoga

  • Beauty/Slimming/Healthcare with upfront payment/packages (exclude merchant dealing with product only)

  • Bridal house

SLA

Entity Origin

SLA

Local Domestic MY Merchants

1WD ( with full requirement fulfilled)

Onboarding Flow

Category

No

Steps

Owner / PIC

Channel Activation

1

Merchants submit requests to apply for Cards payment flow via their own dashboard.

Merchant

2

Receive payment activation request via the #mo-request Slack channel.

Request Example:

MO

3

MO to check merchant’s entity info on the spreadsheet and verify the following inputs

  • Use Case: Merchant must explain how would like to use card payment (e.g. via payment link / ecommerce website)

    • Payment Link Use Case: Merchant must provide a service pricing list and specify payment types. Social media profiles must be Meta-verified (  )to proceed.

Sample verified social media profile :

    • E-commerce Website Use Case: MO must perform re-KYC on the URL to ensure that the website follows the criteria below:

      • with requirement below:

      • Website to have SSM company details (company registered name and company registered number)

      • Website must complete with Terms & condition, privacy policy & refund policy

      • The website must have contact information. Email, address and telephone number

      • Clearly stated the products and pricing in MYR

      • Checkout until the payment page

4

MO to check also the columns E,F & G in the spreadsheet for additional request on cards activation.

  • If column E states, "No", MO can block the foreign card acceptance during the activation process.

  • Column F indicates if merchants would like to enable recurring/auto debit. MO must verify the use case is suitable for recurring features; otherwise, it will not be enabled and resubmission may be requested.

5

If requirements in steps 3 and 4 are unfulfilled or the explanation is unclear, MO needs to send a manual "self-serve" email for more info. Then, monitor responses via #ca-email-notification.

Sample email:

6

If requirements in steps 3 and 4 are fulfilled, can proceed with activation.

Activation Flow

Category

No

Steps

Owner / PIC

Channel Activation

1

Once requirements are fulfilled, MO can proceed with the activation via Channel Activation Retool.

MO

2

Input merchant’s Business ID (BID) and select the latest MY transacting entity (with MY flag)

3

Set Method to CARDS, Activation to SINGLE_ACTIVATION, Channel Code to OCBC and Channel to MPGS OCBC MYR SHARED AGGREGATOR FULL_AMOUNT, then click “Activate”

4

Assign the specific Card MCC based on the merchant’s business nature in the "Merchant" tab (in retool).
Can refer here for guidance: MCC Guideline Mapping

MO needs to ensure that the MID is available to cover the MCC (reference doc of active MID). The MID to cover the MCC must be active to activate cards following the correct MCC. If there is no MID to cover the MCC, MO needs to follow the SOP to request for MID and cards activation must be on hold until the MID created by OCBC.

5

Ensure currency is MYR and cap the single transaction limit at RM5000. Click “next.”

6

Finish activation by clicking “Submit.”

7

Once activation is done via retool, MO to proceed with transaction limit in AD determined by merchant’s Business Segment (MM / SME) following the latest Confluence - Monthly card limit.

8

In the Admin Dashboard (AD), navigate to Customer Management (CM) and search the BID to view details and hover to transaction limit

9

MO to set limit based on merchant business segment then click “save”

10

If the merchant does not intend to accept foreign cards, or MO assigned a mitigation risk to block foreign acceptance on specific merchants upon kyc, MO needs to activate “Block foreign card rule” configuration in AD following the steps in Confluence - Block Foreign Card Rule.

11

If merchant merchant request the recurring/subscription via cards / auto debit and passed the KYC, MO to configure in Channel Activation Retool

  • Tick CREDENTIAL_ON_FILE in the Retool "Merchant" tab to allow MIT then submit request.

  • In "Payment Settings", tick Merchant Initiated Payment and 2DS.

    Note: Do not skip 3DS without Mrisk approval.

Please be reminded that only few MCC are able to perform recurring as per below:

  • MPGS_OCBCMY_7399

  • MPGS_OCBCMY_5999

  • MPGS_OCBCMY_7299

  • MPGS_OCBCMY_4121

  • MPGS_OCBCMY_5732

If the merchant MCC does not support recurring, need to request from OCBC to enable recurring for the MCC / MID (following SOP here).

Retool Example:

12

Once activation and configuration are complete, inform the merchant via the original Slack request thread.

Rejection Flow

Category

No

Steps

Owner / PIC

1.

When a merchant fails to meet the Cards’ onboarding criteria, MO agent is responsible for notifying the merchant directly.

MO

2.

This is a manual "self-serve" process. The MO agent must send a manual email update to the merchant informing them of the rejection result.

3.

All rejection notifications must be sent using the official Merchant Operations email: merchantops@xendit.co.
